headline: Dental Care Wait at Public Clinics Averages 3 Months for Non-Emergency
context: Public dental clinic capacity is overwhelmed with patients waiting months for routine procedures.

cards.big: Average 3-month wait for non-emergency dental treatment at government clinics in 2025
cards.sub: Malaysia has 1 public dentist per 8,200 population versus WHO target of 1 per 3,000

cards.big: Only 48% of Malaysian adults visited a dentist in 2025 versus 72% in developed nations
cards.sub: Access barriers and long waits deter preventive dental care seeking behavior

cards.big: Untreated dental disease costs RM3.8 billion annually in lost productivity and emergency care
cards.sub: Preventive dental care costing RM200 per person avoids RM2,000 in emergency treatment

cards.big: Thailand's dental therapist training program tripled rural dental access within 5 years
cards.sub: Mid-level dental providers can perform routine procedures at lower cost per service

cards.big: Dental health is general health—treating teeth as optional healthcare creates preventable suffering
Should Malaysia train dental therapists to expand routine dental care capacity in underserved areas
